The syllabus is approved for use in England, Wales and Northern Ireland as a Cambridge International Level 1/Level Gomez is the manager of a small computer repair business. Table 1 shows some costs and prices for his business. As it is a competitive market, Gomez knows it is important to maintain customer loyalty and revenue. Gomez knows that a lower break-even point would help improve profits. He thinks the best way to lower the break-even point is to raise prices. Table 1: Cost and price information Price per customer $

question_text: Certificate. 2 0450/11/M/J/15 Country S is enjoying an economic boom. During this time, businesses have increased their profits and unemployment has fallen. The only problem is inflation. All Active is a private limited company. It owns 20 sport and fitness clubs in country S. The Managing Director said: ΟΔβ²§Υ€This is a good time to expand by opening 5 more clubs. Finding the right locations is important. We will need to recruit more employees.ΟΔβ²§β³Δ He cannot decide whether it would be an advantage for All Active to become a public limited company. (a) Identify two stages of the recruitment process. Stage 1: Stage 2: [2] (b) What is meant by an ΟΔβ²§Υ€economic boomΟΔβ²§β³Δ? [2] (c) Explain how falling unemployment might affect All Active. [4] 5 0450/11/M/J/15 [Turn over (d) Identify and explain two factors that All Active should consider when deciding on the location of the new clubs. Factor 1: Explanation: Factor 2: Explanation: [6] (e) Consider the advantages and disadvantages for All Active of being converted to a public limited company. Do you think All Active should become a public limited company? Justify your answer. [6] 6 0450/11/M/J/15

subject: Business Studies
question_text: Si and Ramon are friends. They want to set up a photography business. They would take pictures at weddings, birthdays and other special events. The business will be a partnership. Based on their primary market research, Si believes there is enough demand. Ramon has produced a business plan which shows they need $8000 for a shop and $4000 for equipment. They plan to spend $500 on promotion, but cannot agree on the best method to use. (a) What is meant by a ΟΔβ²§Υ€partnershipΟΔβ²§β³Δ? [2] (b) Identify two methods of primary market research that Si and Ramon might have used. Method 1: Method 2: [2] (c) Identify and explain two possible sources of finance that they could use to obtain the equipment. Source 1: Explanation: Source 2: Explanation: [4] 7 0450/11/M/J/15 [Turn over (d) Identify and explain two advantages to Si and Ramon of having a business plan. Advantage 1: Explanation: Advantage 2: Explanation: [6] (e) Consider two methods of promotion that would be suitable for Si and Ramon to use. Which of these two methods would be the most appropriate? Justify your answer. [6] 8 0450/11/M/J/15

question_text: Javid is a company that manufactures a range of luxury chocolates using batch production. Over the past year, profit has fallen due to increased competition . In order to cut costs, the Finance Director has suggested the company introduces new technology into the production process. The Human Resources Director is not sure that workers would benefit from the introduction of new technology. (a) What is meant by ΟΔβ²§Υ€batch productionΟΔβ²§β³Δ? [2] (b) Identify two advantages (other than cutting costs) to the company of using new technology. Advantage 1: Advantage 2: [2] (c) Identify and explain two reasons why the quality of chocolates might be important to this company. Reason 1: Explanation: Reason 2: Explanation: [4] 9 0450/11/M/J/15 (d) Identify and explain two problems that a fall in profit might cause for the company. Problem 1: Explanation: Problem 2: Explanation: [6] (e) Do you think that the introduction of new technology would benefit JavidΟΔβ²§β³Δs workers? Justify your answer. subject: Business Studies
question_text: Mabel wants to set up her own hairdressing business. She will need to build good customer relationships. She has done some secondary market research to find out possible demand and what methods of promotion to use. She has decided to buy her inventory (stock) from a wholesaler. question_text: May/June 2015 1 hour 30 minutes Candidates answer on the Question Paper. No Additional Materials are required. READ THESE INSTRUCTIONS FIRST Write your Centre number, candidate number and name in the spaces at the top of this page. Write in dark blue or black pen. You may use an HB pencil for any diagrams, graphs or rough working. Do not use staples, paper clips, glue or correction fluid. DO NOT WRITE IN ANY BARCODES. Answer all questions. The businesses described in this question paper are entirely fictitious. At the end of the examination, fasten all your work securely together. The number of marks is given in brackets [ ] at the end of each question or part question. The total number of marks for this paper is 80. The syllabus is approved for use in England, Wales and Northern Ireland as a Cambridge International Level 1/Level GK Hotel is a small hotel in the city centre. It has 15 rooms. Most of its customers are tourists. Last year the number of customers fell due to changes in consumer spending patterns and increased competition. The Marketing manager believes GK must attract customers from different market segments if the hotel is to increase its profits. His plan is to improve the GK website. He is not sure whether he should change the cost plus pricing method he uses. Table 1: Cost and price data for GK Hotel Average price per room $

mark_scheme_text: 3 (a) The company Balance Sheet helps David assess the performance of the business. (i) Calculate the current ratio and acid test ratio for 2014. [4] (ii) Compare your two ratio results with those of 2013 (see Appendix 2) [4] Knowledge [2 ΟÉβÄî 1] ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award one mark for each correct ratio Analysis [2 ΟÉβÄî 1] ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award one mark for each correct calculation (i) Current ratio 1000/600 = 1.66/ 1.67 (1K + 1Ap) Accept 1300/600 = 2.16/ 2.17 if working shown Acid test ratio 700/600 = 1.16/ 1.17 (1K + 1Ap) Accept 1000/600 = 1.6/ 1.67 if working shown 2 marks for each correct answer; 1 mark if correct method/formula but incorrect answer (ii) Application + Analysis [2 ΟÉβÄî 2] ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award one application mark for the comparison between the figures and one analysis mark for each explanation of the calculation result from (i) Indicative response: The current ratio is lower [ap] because the current liabilities have increased [an]. The acid test ratio is higher (ap) because the amount of inventories has fallen so less working capital is being tied up in inventories [an]. NB do not reward ΟΔβ²§Υ€betterΟΔβ²§β³Δ or ΟΔβ²§Υ€worseΟΔβ²§β³Δ for the comparison of the figures but an explanation of why the figure is better or worse can be credited. Knowledge/Analysis/ Evaluation Level 3 At least 2 ΟÉβÄî Level 2 + 9ΟΔβ²§βÄ€10 marks for well justified recommendation as to the best option to choose to increase efficiency compared to the other option. 7ΟΔβ²§βÄ€8 marks for some limited judgement shown in recommendation as to the best option to choose to increase efficiency. mark_scheme_text: 1 (a) Samah and Selina wrote a business plan before FF started trading 5 years ago. Identify and explain two ways their business plan might have helped to make the business successful. [8] Knowledge [2 ΟÉβÄî 1] ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award one mark for each relevant way Analysis [2 ΟÉβÄî 1] ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award one mark for a relevant explanation for each way Application [2 ΟÉβÄî 2] ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award two application marks for each way Award one mark for each relevant way (maximum of two), such as: ΟΔβ²§¬Δ It helps to secure a bank loan or overdraft ΟΔβ²§¬Δ The entrepreneur thinks ahead and plans out carefully the next few years ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Reduces risks of failure as the owners have thought through problems the business might have ΟΔβ²§¬Δ To clarify the aims and objectives / targets to aim for ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Aids financial planning Award a maximum of three additional marks for each explanation ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ 2 of which must be applied to this context - of the way a business plan might help make the business successful. Indicative response: A business plan reduces risk for the business (k) as Samah and Selina will have thought through problems such as where to source their ingredients for the ice cream and what it will cost them (ap). This will allow them to prepare for any problems and stop these causing difficulties for the business (an). This might include preparing for cash flow problems at times of the year when demand is low (ap). Possible application marks: partnership; produces ice cream; high quality ingredients; expensive ingredients; ice cream sold for a high price; borrow finance easily from the bank; sales increased for four years; demand high for 6 months; cost / revenue data from appendix 3; new products / flavours; redundant workers; have taken out a bank loan; high income consumers. There may be other examples in context which have not been included here. Page 3 Mark Scheme Syllabus Paper Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ May/June 2015 0450 22 Ο²¬© Cambridge International Examinations 2015 (b) Consider the advantages to FF of employing full-time workers and the advantages to FF of employing part-time workers. Do you think a combination of these two types of workers would be best? Justify your answer. [12] Relevant points might include: Advantages Full-time workers Likely to be better trained More committed to the business More likely to be trained / skilled than part-time workers Feel secure in job ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ more motivated (although several made redundant) Part-time workers More flexible hours Easier to extend hours at busy times Accept lower pay as fits in with family commitments Knowledge / Analysis / Evaluation ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award up to 10 marks using the levels-based mark scheme below. Knowledge / Analysis / Evaluation Level 3 At least 2 ΟÉβÄî Level 2 + 9ΟΔβ²§βÄ€10 marks for well justified recommendation as to the best type of worker to employ compared to the other type of worker. 7ΟΔβ²§βÄ€8 marks for some limited judgement shown in recommendation as to the best type of worker to employ. Level 2 4ΟΔβ²§βÄ€6 marks Detailed discussion of advantages of part-time and / or full-time workers. Level 1 1ΟΔβ²§βÄ€3

mark_scheme_text: 1 (a) Chris wants to start up a new business. Identify and explain two ways Governments can support new business start-ups. [8] Knowledge [2 ΟÉβÄî 1] ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award one mark for each relevant way Application/Analysis [2 ΟÉβÄî 3] ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award up to three marks for a relevant explanation for each way Award one mark for each relevant way (maximum of two), such as: ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Organise advice ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Provide low cost premises ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Provide loans at low interest rates ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Give grants for capital ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Give grants for training ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Reduced tax payments Award a maximum of three additional marks for each explanation of the ways governments support new business start-ups. Indicative response: Governments may provide advice and support sessions for new business start-ups (k). This advice would help Chris, as he does not have any experience of running a business (ap). Without advice then Chris is more likely to make mistakes and the new business may fail (an). The advice could be from experienced entrepreneurs, which would be particularly helpful in the financial management of a small business (an). mark_scheme_text: 3 (a) Chris knows quality will be very important when producing his products. Identify and explain two ways Chris could achieve quality production. [8] Knowledge [2 ΟÉβÄî 1] ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award one mark for each relevant way Analysis [2 ΟÉβÄî 1] ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award one mark for a relevant explanation for each way Application [2 ΟÉβÄî 2] ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award two application marks for each way Award one mark for each relevant way (maximum of two), such as: ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Quality control ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Quality assurance ΟΔβ²§¬Δ TQM Award a maximum of three additional marks for each explanation ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ 2 of which must be applied to this context ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ of the ways to ensure quality production. Indicative response: Chris could use quality control [k] to check that the ingredients which are purchased are fresh and suitable to use (ap). This will ensure that no poor quality ingredients are used to make the biscuits and therefore there are no unhappy customers (ap). Quality control will be implemented by checking the biscuits at the end of the production line before they are sold to customers (an). Possible application marks: biscuit making; large biscuits; food industry; qualified as a chef/baker; messages such as Happy Birthday; ingredients; high quality biscuits; target market are parents. mark_scheme_text: 4 (a) The Government has increased interest rates to reduce inflation. Identify and explain two ways an increase in interest rates might affect FF. [8] Knowledge [2 ΟÉβÄî 1] award one mark for each relevant way identified Analysis [2 ΟÉβÄî 1] award one mark for a relevant explanation of each way Application [2 ΟÉβÄî 2] award two application marks for each relevant way Award one mark for each relevant way (maximum of two), such as: ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Decreased demand ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ as customers have increased mortgage/loan payments ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Increased cost on new loans ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ less likely to expand ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Increased cost of overdraft ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increased expenses/reduced profit ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Increased costs from higher interest repayments ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ prices of FF services increases/reduces demand. Award a maximum of three additional marks for each explanation ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ two of which must be applied to this context ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ of each of these ways. Indicative response: FF will have increased costs from higher interest repayments on new loans (k) therefore price of photographs may have to rise (ap). This may reduce profit if prices cannot be increased or if prices are increased then demand may fall (an). Other guests at weddings can take photographs so the elasticity of demand may be elastic, leading to falling revenue if prices are increased (ap). NB ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ answer should be clearly related to interest rates increasing and NOT just inflation increasing. 1(a) Identify and explain two ways in which TT might achieve quality production. Knowledge [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 1] ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award one mark for each way. Analysis [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 1] ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award one mark for a relevant explanation for each way. Application [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 2] ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award two application marks for each way. Award one mark for each way (maximum of two), from this list: Ο²βÄΔ Quality control Ο²βÄΔ Quality assurance Ο²βÄΔ Quality labour ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ skilled/trained workers Ο²βÄΔ Quality materials ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ consider producing own T shirts instead of buying in Ο²βÄΔ Use new technology/new computer-controlled equipment/CAD,CAM or CIM Ο²βÄΔ TQM/quality circles Do not accept JIT or lean production. Award a maximum of three additional marks for each explanation ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ 2 of which must be applied to this context - of how the quality can be achieved. Indicative response: Quality control (k) means that each batch of childrenΟΔβ²§β³Δs T-shirts are checked at the end of the production process (app) If there are any faults then the batch can be sewn up and repairs made to any gaps in the seams (app) so that products only leave the factory if they are correctly made and in perfect condition (an). Possible application marks: Ready-made plain white T-shirts; high quality material; prints designs; T-shirts imported from country Q, a developing country; 50 different designs; batches production of various sizes; personalized T-shirts; buying new computer controlled equipment for $500 000; retailers expect high quality products There may be other examples in context that have not been included here. 8 0450/21 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2017 Page 3 of 12 Answer Question Marks

No guarantee of success ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ no expertise in this market. Low staff morale ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ job insecurity ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ lack of coordination between the two management structures ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ diseconomies of scale. Option B ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ expand own production facilities Increased efficiency from new equipment ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increased output ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increased sales. Not using a wholesaler ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ absorb profit margin. Option B is cheaper than Option A. May need joint venture/use an agent ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ reduce profit margin ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ lower profit. Lack of knowledge of export market ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ culture/taste may be different ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ more difficult to have effective marketing. 2(a) Identify two motivational theories and explain how TT could use these theories to help increase worker motivation Knowledge [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 1] ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award one mark for each theory Analysis [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 1] ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award one mark for a relevant explanation for each theory Application [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 2] ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award two application marks for each theory Award one mark for each theory (maximum of two), from: Ο²βÄΔ Taylor ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ scientific management approach, money is the main motivator, pay according to tasks performed so speed is encouraged, specialisation would make best use of skills Ο²βÄΔ Maslow ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ hierarchy of needs, satisfy physiological, safety, social, self-esteem, self- actualisation needs to increase motivation Ο²βÄΔ Herzberg ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ two factor theory where hygiene factors relate to the working environment and motivating factors relate to improving the job itself so that work is meaningful and the worker is given responsibility Note: There may be reference to other motivational theorists. Award a maximum of three additional marks for each explanation ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ 2 of which must be applied to this context ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ of the theory. Note: The explanation must focus on how TT can use each specific theory. Indicative response: Taylor (k) believed that money is a main motivator for workers (an) and so TT should increase the workersΟΔβ²§β³Δ wages especially to pay them for all the tasks they do (app) and then they may feel more motivated and less unhappy in their jobs and not want to leave (app). 3(a) Identify two communication barriers which might occur within TT and explain how each barrier might be reduced. Knowledge [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 1] ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award one mark for each barrier Analysis [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 1] ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award one mark for a relevant explanation for each barrier Application [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 2] ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award two application marks for each barrier Award one mark for each barrier (maximum of two), such as: Ο²βÄΔ Problems with the sender ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ different languages in country P and country Q/ not made the message clear /speaks quickly/sends message to wrong person/too many workers to contact Ο²βÄΔ Problems with the medium ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ message lost/no feedback given/wrong channel used/ message not picked up Ο²βÄΔ Problems with the receiver ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ not listening/ may not trust the sender/not understanding the message Ο²βÄΔ Problems with feedback ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ only one-way communication/there is no feedback/distortion of feedback as passed through too many people/ long chain of command Ο²βÄΔ Problems with the physical environment ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ noise level/separate buildings/time difference Award a maximum of three additional marks for each explanation ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ 2 of which must be applied to this context - of how the barrier might be reduced. 1(a) Identify and explain two ways a business plan could have helped Peter when setting up Value Gym. Indicative response: Peter will have written the business plan to help him gain finance. (ap) The business plan will show financial forecasts to show that the business will be able to repay the loan. (1) Therefore after the bank has given Peter the loan he will have the capital he needs to set up the gym and start signing up members. [Turn over 06_0450_21_2019_1.1 *0703178251-I* Soap Additions (SA) SA is a public limited company. It was started 20 years ago in country Z. SA manufactures soap that is sold to the mass market. The soap is produced in a factory employing 100 production workers. The Operations manager thinks that well-motivated employees are important. SA soap is sold for a low price to wholesalers and retailers throughout country Z. The product is sold and packaged either as bars of soap or liquid soap in a bottle. SA has a 50% share of the total market for low price soap. The directors of SA want to expand the business by starting to produce different types of soap aimed at niche markets. The products they want to develop are: ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Luxury soap aimed at high income consumers ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Soaps aimed at improving ageing skin ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Medicated soap to help improve bad skin often experienced by teenagers The manufacturing process for the mass produced soap is automated and uses flow production. However, if the new soaps are produced then they will be manufactured in a new factory using batch production. The new factory and equipment will cost $15m and will require 50 more production workers and a new Operations manager. SA does not use lean production in its existing factory. However, the Managing Director thinks SA would benefit from the introduction of lean production into the existing and the new factory. Some of SAΟΔβ²§β³Δs managers think they are never involved when important decisions are made. The directors of SA disagree about which leadership style will lead to the best decisions being taken. 06_0450_21_2019_1.1 2 Appendix Newspaper article in Daily News -

This could reduce the market price of their shares in the soap company (ap) as it will take time for the new investment to lead to higher profits and possibly higher dividends (an). 3(a) Identify and explain two ways SA could use lean production in its factory. Knowledge [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 1] Award one mark for each relevant way Analysis [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 1] Award one mark for an explanation for each way Application [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 2] Award two application marks for each way Ο²βÄΔ Just-in-time/JIT ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ inventory arrives at production line when wanted/ no warehousing of inventory Ο²βÄΔ Kaizen ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ continuous improvement/eliminates waste/ better layout of factory improves flow of production Ο²βÄΔ Cell production ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ small team working/responsible for complete unit of work Ο²βÄΔ Kanban ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ inventory arrives at production line only when inventory has been used/reduces the holding of inventory Indicative response: Just-in-time (k) means that inventory is reduced to no or very low levels held in storage. (an) This reduces the need for having warehouse space to hold the ingredients to make soap. (ap) SA will need to have good relationships with its suppliers to ensure that the flow production line is not interrupted by inventory running out. (ap) Possible application marks: Produces soap; sold in a mass market; wants to start selling niche market soaps; employs 100 production workers; flow production; batch production. There may be other examples in context that have not been included here. 8 0450/21 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2019 Page 10 of 14 Question Answer Marks

mark_scheme_text:
1(a) Identify and explain one advantage and one disadvantage to WA of developing new products. Knowledge ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award one mark for each relevant advantage / disadvantage [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 1 marks] Analysis ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award one mark for a relevant explanation for each advantage / disadvantage [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 1 marks] Application ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award two application marks for each advantage / disadvantage [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 2 marks] Answers are likely to include: Advantage Ο²βÄΔ New markets Ο²βÄΔ Maintains image for fashionable designs Ο²βÄΔ Remains competitive Ο²βÄΔ Expansion Ο²βÄΔ Wider range of products / diversification / spreading risk Ο²βÄΔ Attract more customers / increase sales Disadvantage Ο²βÄΔ New products might fail Ο²βÄΔ May not be demanded if not liked by consumers Ο²βÄΔ Increased costs ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ for example development costs / marketing / advertising / market research / storage or warehouse costs / wasted raw materials during development / training labour in new skills Ο²βÄΔ Higher prices needed to recoup development costs Indicative response: WA will remain competitive (k) by ensuring the furniture is fashionable (ap). This will increase demand or at least maintain demand for its furniture (an) and may result in profit increasing again (ap). Possible application marks: Family owned ltd company; small business; started 10 years ago; furniture; wood from sustainable forests; uses batch production to make furniture; new fashionable designs each year; selling price $200; environmentally friendly business objective; reduced profit in 2018; chairs / tables 8 0450/22 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2019 Page 4 of 14 Question Answer Marks

2(a) Identify and explain two examples of tertiary sector businesses that help WA to be successful. Knowledge ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award one mark for identifying an example of a tertiary sector business [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 1 marks] AnalysisΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award one mark for a relevant explanation of how the tertiary sector business helps WA be successful [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 1 marks] Application ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award two application marks for explaining in context how the tertiary sector business helps WA to be successful [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 2 marks] Answers are likely to include: Ο²βÄΔ Bank Ο²βÄΔ Insurance Ο²βÄΔ Advertising agency Ο²βÄΔ Transport services Ο²βÄΔ Communication e.g. mobile phone networks, internet providers Ο²βÄΔ IT services Ο²βÄΔ Retailer / wholesaler e.g. furniture retailer Ο²βÄΔ Training companies Indicative response: Transport services (k) that delivers furniture to customers (ap). This saves WA having to purchase its own vehicles to deliver tables and chairs (ap) and so saves WA the expense of delivery vehicles when they would not be used all the time (an). Possible application marks: Family owned ltd; small business; started 10 years ago; furniture; wood from sustainable forests; new fashionable designs each year; individually designed furniture produced; chairs / tables; reduced profit in 2018. mark_scheme_text:
3(a) Identify and explain how two stakeholder groups of WA are likely to be affected by the reduction in profit in 2018. Knowledge ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award one mark for identifying a relevant stakeholder group [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 1 marks] Analysis ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award one mark for a relevant explanation how each stakeholder group will be affected [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 1 marks] Application ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ award two application marks for explaining in context how each stakeholder group will be affected [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 1 marks] Answers are likely to include: Shareholder / Directors / Owners / Family Ο²βÄΔ Lower dividend likely to be paid ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ lower return on their investment Ο²βÄΔ Need to change business objectives to try to increase profit again ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ may require further investment ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ changes to the way the company is operated Employees OR accept workers and managers as separate stakeholders Ο²βÄΔ Less likely to be given a wage increase ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ less motivated Ο²βÄΔ May have to accept a reduction in wage rates or longer hours for same pay Suppliers Ο²βÄΔ Asked to reduce price of wood ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ less wood purchased Ο²βÄΔ Slower to get paid for wood supplied ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ worse cash flow Accept other stakeholder groups e.g. customers, competitors, government, bank, local community. Indicative response: Supplier (k) that sells wood to WA to make the furniture (ap) may not be paid as quickly if the profit is lower (an). Supplier may have to lower the price of its wood to help WA reduce its costs (ap). Possible application marks: Family owned ltd company; small business; started 10 years ago; furniture; wood from sustainable forests; selling price $200 and $250 per item; environmentally friendly business objective; 2 brothers and a sister; 20 employees; 10% above minimum wage. There may be other examples in context that have not been included here. Note ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Do NOT reward reduction in profit as application because this is in the question. 8 0450/22 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2019 Page 10 of 14 Question Answer Marks

3(b) Consider the following three ways WA could use to increase employee motivation. Recommend which way WA should choose. Justify your answer. Relevant points might include: Introduce piece rate Ο²βÄΔ TaylorΟΔβ²§β³Δs theory ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ money motivates Ο²βÄΔ Increases pay ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ by rewards for producing a higher output However Ο²βÄΔ Wage costs may increase Ο²βÄΔ Quality might be reduced if rush to produce more output Ο²βÄΔ Less likely to be applicable to office staff and so may not be fair Give shares to WA employees Ο²βÄΔ Employees now receive payment from dividends Ο²βÄΔ Increased rewards from higher output / profit Ο²βÄΔ Maslow ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increased sense of belonging However Ο²βÄΔ Existing shareholders of this private limited company will lose some control Ο²βÄΔ May lead to reduced dividends per share or less retained profit Ο²βÄΔ Shares may not easily be sold (Ltd) ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ workers may prefer cash to shares Introduce job rotation Ο²βÄΔ Makes the work more interesting / less boring Ο²βÄΔ Change tasks performing regularly However Ο²βÄΔ Increases training requirements as each worker currently only performs one task Ο²βÄΔ Quality may be lower if less specialised

4(a)(i) Calculate the total cost per month of producing 100 items of furniture. Award 1 mark for correct costs and 1 mark for correct answer $10 000 + $1 200 + $800 + $4 000 (1) = $16 000 (1) Note ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ if answer is only $16 000 with no working then award 2 marks 2

4(a)(ii) Calculate the average cost of 1 piece of furniture. Award 1 mark for correct figures used and 1 mark for correct answer $16 000 / 100 (1) = $160 per item of furniture (1) Note ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ If answer is only $160 with no working then award 2 marks 2

4(a)(iii) Explain how total cost and average cost will change if the output is increased to 200 items of furniture per month. Award 1 mark per point: Total cost will increase due to increase in variable costs (1) ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ as more wood will need to be purchased (1) TC = $26 000 / increased by $10 000 (1) Average cost will fall (1) ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ as the fixed costs will not change (1) ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ which means the fixed costs are spread over a larger output. (1) AC = $130 per item of furniture / decreased by $30 (1) Total cost will increase whilst average cost will fall (1) (Apply OFR) Note ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ 4 marks can be awarded even if no figures are included. 4 0450/22 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2019 Page 13 of 14 Question Answer Marks

4(b) Consider how the following three changes might affect WA. Which change is likely to have the biggest effect on WA? Justify your answer. Relevant points might include: Introduction of new cost to businesses of $50 per tonne for removing waste Ο²βÄΔ Increased cost of removing waste ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ higher prices ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ less demand for furniture ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ lower profit Ο²βÄΔ Applies to all businesses so not less competitive ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ if reduce waste produced then may be more competitive Increase in legal minimum wage by 5% Ο²βÄΔ No effect as already paid 10% above minimum wage Ο²βÄΔ Increased wage costs ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ to keep pay differential ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increased total cost ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ lower profit Ο²βÄΔ Increased income ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increased demand for furniture ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ higher sales and profit Ο²βÄΔ May increase motivation if increase in wage rates for employees Increase in tax on incomes Ο²βÄΔ Lower incomes as pay after tax will be lower ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ lower demand ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ lower sales of furniture Ο²βÄΔ WA furniture is higher quality so may not have much effect on sales Ο²βÄΔ Workers may demand higher wages to compensate for loss of disposable income 12 0450/22 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2019 Page 14 of 14 Question Answer Marks

4(b) Award up to 10 marks using the levels-based mark scheme below. Knowledge / Analysis / Evaluation Level 3 At least 2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ Level 2 + 9ΟΔβ²§βÄ€10 marks for well justified conclusion as to which change will have the greatest effect on WA rather than the other two changes. 7ΟΔβ²§βÄ€8 marks for limited justified conclusion as to which change will have the greatest effect on WA. Level 2 4ΟΔβ²§βÄ€6 marks Discussion of the impact of the changes on WA. Level 1 1ΟΔβ²§βÄ€3 marks Outline of impact of changes on WA. Knowledge [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 1] Award one mark for each relevant reason Analysis [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 1] Award one mark for an explanation of each reason Application [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 2] Award two application marks for each reason Answers are likely to include: Ο²βÄΔ Getting to know customersΟΔβ²§β³Δ wants by asking them ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ makes them loyal to the business ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ retains customers and reputation Ο²βÄΔ To identify what customers want from the van conversion ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Peter can adapt the service to meet customer needs Ο²βÄΔ Peter wants to maintain a good reputation ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ so customers tell others about the business and increase advertising by word of mouth ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increase sales Ο²βÄΔ Increase customer loyalty Indicative response: By having a good relationship with customers Peter can ask them about what they want (k). This means Peter must find out what sort of van conversions customers might want. (ap) If Peter then offers these services he will be more likely to attract customers to VC (an) and the business will continue to grow as this is what Peter wants as his main business objective. (ap) Possible application marks: Motorhome; van conversion; changes vans and large cars; sole trader business; started 2 years ago; plans to expand by offering additional services; financial information from Appendix 3; main business objective is growth; job production; every vehicle is different. There may be other examples in context that have not been included here. 8 0450/23 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2019 Page 10 of 14 Question Answer Marks

4(a) Identify and explain one advantage and one disadvantage to VC of using job production. Knowledge [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 1] Award one mark for each relevant advantage/disadvantage Analysis [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 1] Award one mark for an explanation of each advantage/disadvantage Application [2 ΟΔβÄΙβÄΠ 2] Award two application marks for each advantage/disadvantage Answers are likely to include: Advantages: Ο²βÄΔ It is suitable for these one-off personalised van conversions ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ each one is different according to customer preferences ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ can charge higher prices Ο²βÄΔ The service meets the exact requirements of the customer ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increasing customer satisfaction Ο²βÄΔ More interesting and varied work for employees ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ can be more motivating than flow or batch production Disadvantages: Ο²βÄΔ Likely for higher wages to be paid to skilled workers ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ costs are higher Ο²βÄΔ Labour-intensive production often takes longer ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ as each job is different ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ it may take more time to complete a customer order Ο²βÄΔ Materials used may need to be ordered separately ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ take time to arrive as made especially to order and any mistakes will be expensive to replace Ο²βÄΔ Cannot easily be sold to someone else ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ materials may have to be specially purchased ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increasing costs Indicative response: Service meets the exact requirements of the customer (k) so increases customer satisfaction leading to good image for the business and increase sales. (an) The vans will be converted in the exact way the customer wants (ap) and then when travelling round other people will see the van and if they like the inside, such as a cooker included, then they may be more likely to ask VC to undertake a conversion for them. (ap) Possible application marks: Motorhome; van conversion; changes vans and large cars; sole trader business; started 2 years ago; plans to expand by offering additional services; needs 3 new skilled and experienced workers; financial information from appendix 3; ordering car parts. There may be other examples in context that have not been included here. 8 0450/23 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2019 Page 13 of 14 Question Answer Marks

1(a) Explain four reasons for changes in the relative importance of the primary, secondary and tertiary sectors in a country. One mark for each reason for the change (maximum of 2). Award a maximum of 1 additional mark for each explanation. Note: There are no application marks for this question. Relevant reasons might include: ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Change in the primary sector ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increase due to new discovery of natural resources / decreases due to depletion of natural resources ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Change in the secondary sector ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increase due to industrialisation / decreases due to deindustrialisation ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Change in the tertiary sector ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increase due to increased consumer incomes ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Increase in incomes / economic growth / increase in GDP ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ this could lead to a change in the secondary or tertiary sector ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Increase in demand for manufactured goods ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increase in the size of secondary sector ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Increase in tertiary sector ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ as consumers have higher disposable income to spend on services ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Increased mechanisation / increased technology ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ reduces number of employees in the secondary sector ΟΔβ²§¬Δ People move into the tertiary sector to provide services ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ as demand for services grows as a country develops e.g. tourism ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Increase in exported products ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ more need to be manufactured in secondary sector ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Reduced employment in primary sector ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ as now primary products produced using mechanisation / rural to urban migration ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Primary products are now processed in own country ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increased manufacturers / secondary sector to process the raw materials into finished products ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Government policies to encourage development ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Increased globalisation ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Actions of environmental groups / pressure groups leading to sectoral change ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ primary extraction or dirty manufacturing in the secondary sector For example: Incomes have increased (1) and consumers have more purchasing power for manufactured goods increasing the importance of the secondary sector (1). 8 0450/21 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2021 Page 6 of 17 Question Answer Marks

2(a) Explain one benefit and one limitation to Yasin of using a break-even chart. Award 1 mark for each benefit/limitation (maximum of two marks). Award a maximum of 3 additional marks for each explanation of the benefit/limitation of using the break-even chartΟΔβ²§βÄ€ one of which must be applied to this context. Relevant benefits might include: Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Shows break-even output ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ so Yasin knows how much bread he needs to sell to break even ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ this will allow him to plan the bakery and equipment required ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ to ensure at least the minimum quantity of bread is produced and sold Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Shows the margin of safety ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ this is the amount of bread Yasin predicts will be sold above the break-even output ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ it provides a target for sales of bread ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ so that MB makes a profit Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Helps in decision making ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ so that Yasin knows the minimum target amount of bread to produce ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ he can see if he needs to increase the price to break-even more quickly ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ or if lower ingredient costs is required by MB to make a profit Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Shows the area of profit or loss ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ and therefore what the output needs to be to cover all costs ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ if the market research shown in the business plan does not indicate that sufficient sales will be made to make a profit ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ then Yasin may need to think again about his plans for the bakery. Relevant limitations might include: Ο·βÄö¬Ζ A break-even chart assumes all the output will be sold/provides an estimate of sales ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ but MB may not sell all the bread in the day ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ some may be wasted and the costs of producing these will not be taken into account ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ so at a specific output the business may not make a profit but may make a loss Ο·βÄö¬Ζ The break-even chart is drawn on the assumption of straight lines ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ but when the output is higher Yasin may be able to bulk buy ingredients ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ this may mean he can receive discounts so the variable cost per unit may not be constant Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Fixed costs/variable costs may change ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ if the scale of production changes as the bakery expands ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ the rent or depreciation of equipment will change/ingredient costs may change Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Need a separate break-even chart for each product/need a new chart if prices are changed ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ relevant if going to make cakes For example: The chart shows the break-even output for MB (1) so Yasin will know how many units of bread he will need to sell each month to break even (app). This will allow him to plan for the equipment required (1) so he can ensure the bakery sells the minimum amount of bread to cover all the costs and not make a loss (1). Application could include: bread; bakery; cakes; data presented in Appendix 2; fixed costs will change if either of the options in Appendix 2 go ahead. 8 0450/21 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2022 Page 9 of 18 Question Answer Marks

1(a) Explain two drawbacks for EP of having a tall organisational structure. Award 1 mark for each drawback (max 2). Award a maximum of 3 additional marks for each explanation of the drawback of having a tall organisational structure ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ one of which must be applied to this context. Relevant drawbacks might include: Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Communication is slower ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ as a long chain of command ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ takes longer for messages to pass from senior managers to lowest level employees Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Misunderstandings may occur ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ as more people for messages to be passed through ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ more chance of errors being passed on Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Senior managers are more remote from lower levels in the hierarchy ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ these managers are less aware of the views of employees ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ more likely for employees to feel demotivated Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Decision making can be slower/weaker ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ as more levels to discuss issues and pass on decisions ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ react to customer changes more slowly Ο·βÄö¬Ζ May have a narrow span of control ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ which may reduce the opportunities for delegation ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ employees feel less trusted with more direct control Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Increasing managerial/supervisor costs ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ as likely to need more managers as there are more levels in the hierarchy For example: Communication is slower (1) as more employees for messages to be passed through (1) giving more chance of errors in the messages (1) about the production of pottery (app). Application could include: pottery; cups; plates; bowls; trading for 40 years; new Marketing Manager; Managing Director; 84 employees; 6 levels; external recruitment/recruitment from outside the business; high-quality products; want to increase efficiency; private limited company; information from Appendix 2. 8 0450/21 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2024 Ο²¬© Cambridge University Press & Assessment 2024 Page 7 of 21 Question Answer Marks

4(a) Explain two factors EP should consider when choosing a source of finance. Award 1 mark for each factor (max 2). Award a maximum of 3 additional marks for each explanation of a factor that affects which source of finance to choose ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ one of which must be applied to this context . Relevant answers might include: Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Legal form of business ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ EP is a private limited company so share issue is an option ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ and new shares can be sold to raise finance Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Amount required ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ if the finance is required to improve cash flow then it may not be a very large amount ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ and it is likely sources such as an overdraft will provide sufficient funds Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Length of time to repay ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ if the finance is required for a short length of time then trade credit might be suitable ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ as this is repaid to the supplier within 30/60 days ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ if needed for long period of time then other sources may be more suitable Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Existing loans ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ if the business already has several loans, then this may not be an option ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ due to the already high expense of interest payments on the loans Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Control of the business ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ the original owners may not want to lose control and therefore may not choose to sell more shares ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ so the control and ownership of the business remains with the original owners Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Purpose for using the finance Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Size of business Ο·βÄö¬Ζ The interest cost involved with some sources of finance ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ which will increase costs and lower profit margins For example: The purpose for using the finance. (1) If the finance is required for ways to improve efficiency at EP (app) then a source such as a bank loan could be chosen (1) as this finance can be used to increase efficiency and then repaid over a longer period of time as unit costs are reduced and profitability increases (1). Application could include: pottery; cups; plates; bowls; trading for 40 years; private limited company; high-quality products; want sales to increase; plastic or bamboo products; wants to increase efficiency; improving labour skills; more automation and technology; information from Appendix 2. 8 0450/21 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2024 Ο²¬© Cambridge University Press & Assessment 2024 Page 19 of 21 Question Answer Marks

Award a maximum of 3 additional marks for each explanation of the reason why setting business objectives is important ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ one of which must be applied to this context. Relevant reasons might include: Ο·βÄö¬Ζ A clear target/aim/purpose/goal to work towards ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ guides the business in the right direction ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ avoids loss of focus Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Decision-making will be focused on the objectives ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ meaning better decisions are taken ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ the business may be more efficient Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Helps motivate employees ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ objectives will help focus the employees and management to increase efficiency ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ making it more likely to be achieved Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Comparison of performance ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ so business managers can compare how each department has performed against their objectives - to see whether or not it has been successful Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Allows measurement of success ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ can judge performance or progress Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Helps the business to produce a plan or strategy for the business to achieve these objectives For example: To give a clear target for employees and managers to work towards (1) so they all know what is expected of them (1) when producing different items of jewellery (app). Therefore, this means the target is more likely to be achieved (1). 4(a) Using Appendix 2 and other information, explain two ways JJ may be affected by the forecast increase in unemployment in country X. Award 1 mark for each way (max 2). Award a maximum of 3 additional marks for each explanation of the way JJ may be affected by the forecast increase in unemployment ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ one of which must be applied to this context. Relevant ways might include: Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Makes it easier for JJ to recruit new employees ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ larger pool of unemployed people to choose from ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ may be more skilled people available for work Ο·βÄö¬Ζ May make it easier to resist demand for higher wages from employees ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ rising level of unemployment means more competition amongst workers for jobs - so willing to accept lower wages Ο·βÄö¬Ζ May lead to lower demand for JJ's products ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ consumer spending is lower ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ as many people have lost their jobs so incomes are lower Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Lower priced products produced by JJ may see an increase in demand ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ as consumers have less income available to spend on luxury high priced items Ο·βÄö¬Ζ JJ may be less willing to invest ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ as future sales may not increase for some time ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ making it more difficult to gain a return on investment Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Lower sales in country X so JJ may try harder to export its products - spreads risk For example: May make it easier for JJ to recruit new employees (1) as there is a larger pool of unemployed people to choose from (1) and many of these workers may be skilled people available for work (1) with experience of how to manufacture jewellery (app). Application could include: jewellery; set up 25 years ago; public limited company; objective to increase profit every year; to expand the business; sold directly to customers in other countries; 30% sold using ecommerce; 70% sold in jewellery shops; raw materials purchased locally; 60 full-time employees; 25 part-time employees; bracelets; rings; earrings; increase from 3% to 6%. 8 0450/22 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2024 Ο²¬© Cambridge University Press & Assessment 2024 Page 17 of 18 Question Answer Marks

3(a) Using Appendix 2 and other information, explain two benefits and two limitations to Santhosh of using break-even analysis. Award one mark for each benefit/limitation identified (max 4). Award a maximum of 1 additional mark for each explanation in context (max 4). Benefits might include: Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Provides a target output RF needs to sell before a profit is made ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ at the break-even output of 10 items Ο·βÄö¬Ζ The impact of business decisions can be shown by redrawing the chart ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ if the variable costs increase from $4 per month Ο·βÄö¬Ζ It can be used to show a margin of safety to protect against losses if a fall in sales occurs ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ from the current margin of safety of 10 Limitations might include: Ο·βÄö¬Ζ The break-even chart is drawn assuming all goods produced are sold - forecast sales are 20 items of furniture per month Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Fixed costs will only remain fixed if the scale of production does not change ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ at $50 per month Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Revenue may not be the same ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ RF may decide to offer a 10% discount Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Can only be used for a single product ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ RF sells a range of furniture and not a single item priced the at the same price Ο·βÄö¬Ζ It assumes that variable/total costs can be drawn as straight lines For example: It will provide a target of how much RF needs to sell each month before they make a profit (1). 4(a) Explain two benefits to RF of having a well-motivated workforce. Award 1 mark for each benefit of having a well-motivated workforce (max 2). Award a maximum of 3 additional marks for each explanation of the benefit of having a well-motivated workforce is important ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ one of which must be applied to this context. Benefits might include: Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Low labour turnover ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ a loyal workforce ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ reduces costs of recruitment Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Low rate of absenteeism ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ reduces disruption caused by an employee absent from work ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ more able to meet demand Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Low rates of industrial action ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ avoiding damage to customer relations - maintaining reputation Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Increases productivity ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ fewer employees are needed to achieve the same level of output Ο·βÄö¬Ζ Higher quality goods and services ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increased customer loyalty ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increases revenue Ο·βÄö¬Ζ More competitive ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ which may increase market share For example: Increases productivity (1) RF will have 10 employees (app) so they can each produce more which reduces the need for additional employees (1) which helps to reduce labour costs (1). Application could include: new/start-up business, carpenter, school leavers, small business, sole trader, $5000 start-up capital, furniture, low-income families, 10% discount, free delivery, figures from Appendix 2, recession. 8 0450/23 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2024 Ο²¬© Cambridge University Press & Assessment 2024 Page 18 of 20 Question Answer Marks

Detailed discussion of two or more methods of communication. Well-justified recommendation. Candidates discussing all three methods of communication in detail, in context and with a well-justified recommendation, including why the alternative methods were rejected, should be rewarded with the top marks in the band. 9ΟΔβ²§βÄ€12 2 Sound application of knowledge and understanding of relevant business concepts using appropriate terminology. Detailed discussion of at least one method of communication. Judgement with some justification/some evaluation of choices made. Candidates discussing two or more methods of communication in detail and applying them to the case should be rewarded with the top marks in the band. 5ΟΔβ²§βÄ€8 1 Limited application of knowledge and understanding of relevant business concepts. Limited ability to discuss the methods of communication with little/no explanation. Simple judgement with limited justification/limited evaluation of choices made. 1(b) Relevant points might include: Paying bonuses ΟΔβ²§¬Δ M ay be flexible in the way it is paid ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ higher pay if the production team increase output of shoes ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ ensuring that they each work harder so the team has an increased payment ΟΔβ²§¬Δ May bring pay up to the same rate paid by local businesses for similar work ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ employees less likely to leave ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ lowering PFΟΔβ²§β³Δs recruitment costs ΟΔβ²§¬Δ May cause disagreements if only some of the 20 employees are working hard ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ may be seen as unfair if all get the same bonus while not all the employees work as hard as each other ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Paying a bonus is likely to mean profits for the business are lower than expected ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ may limit retained profit for expansion ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Can become expected and if not paid then employees become demotivated ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ reducing productivity Piece - rate ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Wages paid to employees will increase if there is a higher output, so workers will be motivated to increase their output and produce more shoes in a shorter time ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increasing efficiency ΟΔβ²§¬Δ If work is rushed to obtain more wages, then quality of the shoes might suffer ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ leading to a poor reputation for the business ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ fewer repeat customers ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ lower revenue in the future ΟΔβ²§¬Δ May be hard to calculate the contribution of each employee to the total number of shoes produced ΟΔβ²§¬Δ It may be seen as unfair by employees if different jobs produce more output than others ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ then motivation may reduce rather than increase 0450 / 21 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2025 Ο²¬© Cambridge University Press & Assessment 2025 Page 9 of 20 Question Answer Marks

1(b) Job rotation ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Production workers carry out one task for a period of time and then swap round to carry out another task using natural rubber to make the job more interesting and less boring ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ provides a greater variety of work ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Makes rotating the workers around the new factory easier ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ so can fill in for colleagues who are absent ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ means less likely to have hold - ups in production ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ output produced on time ΟΔβ²§¬Δ May require training for employees which increases costs ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ but once trained the employee should be more productive ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ a one - off cost that will not keep being paid Recommendation Justification might include: ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Paying a bonus is the best way to motivate the 20 employees as this will add extra to their wages for the work they do producing shoes and encourage them to work harder. Paying piece - rate may be difficult to calculate each week as employees do different jobs, and some may take longer than others. Job rotation may be difficult to introduce if the tasks are very different. ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Paying workers using piece - rate is the best way to motivate employees as this means that employees who work harder producing shoes will be rewarded with higher wages which will make them feel it is all worth the effort, based on TaylorΟΔβ²§β³Δs theory of motivation. ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Job rotation is the best way to motivate employees as this is the only one which makes the job of producing shoes more interesting and less boring . Money motivates only for a short

2(b) Relevant points might include: Availability of raw materials ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Natural rubber is available locally then this will reduce the transport costs ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ reducing PFΟΔβ²§β³Δs costs ΟΔβ²§¬Δ May make supplying extra raw materials at short notice easier ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ especially if an urgent order is taken ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ customer satisfaction more likely to be assured so customers may be more loyal ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ maintaining a regular source of revenue ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Forests are nearby then likely to have better relationship with suppliers ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ may be more willing to give trade credit Transport links ΟΔβ²§¬Δ If transport links are good, finished shoes are likely to reach the market more quickly ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ reducing transport costs for deliveries ΟΔβ²§¬Δ If transport links are good delivery vehicles will be less likely to be stuck in traffic/congestion which increases costs of paying the driver and the fuel for the vehicle ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ quicker to access raw materials especially if PF uses JIT ΟΔβ²§¬Δ May be easier for employees to get to work ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ less likely to be late / arrive on time for shift ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ more efficient production ΟΔβ²§¬Δ May be able to attract employees from a wider area if transport links are good ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ may be easier to recruit enough workers ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ making it easier to start production at the new factory 0450 / 21 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2025 Ο²¬© Cambridge University Press & Assessment 2025 Page 13 of 20 Question Answer Marks

2(b) Government influence ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Government grants may be available to businesses that locate in a particular area ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ which reduces the costs of setting up the new factory in that area ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Often offered in areas that have high levels of unemployment ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ so there may be many people willing and able to work so easier to find employees for the new factory ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Government grants may be given to help with training costs to produce shoes ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ so training costs may not be as high as expected ΟΔβ²§¬Δ There may be restrictions on the use of the land or emissions from the factory that may affect its operation Conclusion Justification might include: ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Availability of natural rubber as without raw materials PF cannot produce any output. So , it is essential that raw materials are nearby so the factory can always operate. Transport links are less important if the market is nearby as delays will not cause major problems if the journey is short. Government influence may only be important at the set - up stage of the factory so will not be helpful in the long run. ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Good transport links are essential as delays in deliveries of shoes to the market may lead to a decrease in customer loyalty. ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Government influence is most important as grants can significantly reduce the set - up and training costs for employees to manufacture shoes making it more likely to be a success. 0450 / 21 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2025 Ο²¬© Cambridge University Press & Assessment 2025 Page 14 of 20 Question Answer Marks 3 ( a ) Explain two advantages and two disadvantages to a business of using a bank loan as a source of finance. 3(b) Relevant points might include: Advantages Disadvantages Leaflets handed out in the street ΟΔβ²§¬Δ A cheap method of advertising shoes ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ keep s marketing budget costs lower ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Can be given out to a wide range of people ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ may attract potential customers who may not otherwise see the advertisement ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Can be kept for future reference ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ so are more likely to remember the details of the rubber shoes ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ and may therefore be more likely to make a purchase in the future ΟΔβ²§¬Δ May not be read ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ and just thrown away in the street ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ wasted cost of producing the leaflets ΟΔβ²§¬Δ May be seen as junk and can be annoying for potential customer of shoes ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ so ignore it and not make a purchase ΟΔβ²§¬Δ May lead to litter and cause pollution in the streets leading to a bad reputation for PF Billboards on the side of a main road ΟΔβ²§¬Δ A permanent form of advertising for a period of time ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ more likely to be seen more than once and therefore attract the attention of passers by ΟΔβ²§¬Δ They can potentially be seen by a large number of people ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ walking or driving past the billboard ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ may attract the attention of many potential customers ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Can easily be missed ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ especially if the person is driving by and concentrating on the road/traffic ΟΔβ²§¬Δ No detailed information about the sustainable sources of raw materials can be included in the billboard ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ as only large writing can be seen, and so only brief general message can be on the billboard ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Possibly higher cost of advertising compared to the other two for the shoe manufacturer 0450 / 21 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2025 Ο²¬© Cambridge University Press & Assessment 2025 Page 17 of 20 Question Answer Marks

3(b) Advantages Disadvantages Discounts on first purchase ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Discount s make the shoes more affordable ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ which can encourage potential customers to make the first purchase ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ may be tempted to buy when otherwise wouldnΟΔβ²§β³Δt ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increasing sales ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Lower price may make them more competitive ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increas ing sales of the new product in this competitive market ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Business receives a lower price than otherwise would ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ lower profit margin ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Unless the discount is quite high then may not be sufficient to encourage potential customers to purchase new shoes Conclusion Justification could include: ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Leaflets handed out in the street will be the most effective because they can be kept by potential customers and reminded of the shoes so a purchase can be made later. The name of the product and where it can be purchased will not be forgotten so sales are likely to increase. Billboards will be less effective as they can easily be missed as people go past them and a discount will only be effective if it is a large discount, which will significantly reduce PFΟΔβ²§β³Δs profit margin. ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Billboards on the side of the road will be the most effective way to increase sales because it will be seen every day by a large number of people passing by and if the billboard is attractive, it will draw the attention of many potential customers leading to higher sales of shoes . mark_scheme_text: 4(b) Relevant points might include: Basic points might include: ΟΔβ²§¬Δ The non - current assets are forecast to increase by $500 000 ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Inventories are forecast to increase by $20 000 ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Long - term bank loans are forecast to increase by $460 000 ΟΔβ²§¬Δ The amount of retained profit is forecast to increase by $40 000 to $100 000 in 2026 More developed points might include: ΟΔβ²§¬Δ The current ratio for 2025 is 1.25 ΟΔβ²§¬Δ The current ratio forecast for 2026 is 1.2 ΟΔβ²§¬Δ The acid test ratio for 2025 is 1 ΟΔβ²§¬Δ The acid test ratio forecast for 2026 is 0.6 ΟΔβ²§¬Δ The current ratio for 2025 could be considered low and is better to be nearer 2 which would show that the business is able to pay its short - term debts and has sufficient liquidity ΟΔβ²§¬Δ The current ratio forecast for 2026 is lower than for 2025 and is a cause for concern as to why it has fallen. This may mean that the business will have expanded too quickly and may have cash flow problems in the future ΟΔβ²§¬Δ The low acid test ratio for 2026 suggests that the business is likely to have liquidity problems after the new factory is built ΟΔβ²§¬Δ The expansion appears to have been forecast to be mainly funded by a large bank loan of $460 000 ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ this may cause problems in the future as the interest repayments of this loan will result in a large increase in expenses ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ possibly reducing profitability i n the future Recommendation Justification might include: ΟΔβ²§¬Δ PhilipΟΔβ²§β³Δs sister should invest in PF as the non - current assets are forecast to increase by nearly double and the building of the new factory is predicted to be financed mainly by a bank loan. The bank will be confident of a successful expansion of PF, or it will not grant such a big loan. This should help reassure PhilipΟΔβ²§β³Δs sister that the investment will provide good returns in the future. ΟΔβ²§¬Δ PhilipΟΔβ²§β³Δs sister should not invest in PF as the forecast statement of financial position suggests that there may be significant liquidity problems producing shoes in the future with an expected fall in the current ratio of 0.05 and an expected fall in the acid test ratio of 0.4.

2(b) Relevant points might include: Advantages Disadvantages Improving labour skills ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Increases motivation of skilled production employees ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ made to feel more valued as training given to them ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ investment in the employees ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Increases skills of employees so may enable them to work more quickly ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ output of sports equipment is produced in a shorter time ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Output per worker increases ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increases productivity ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ fewer inputs for a given output ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Employees may become more flexible ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ with training they could possibly move around the factory covering for absent colleagues ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ keeps the factory running smoothly/fewer interruptions in output ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Increases costs of making footballs ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ in the short - term may reduce profitability ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ if increase in costs is greater than increase in output when the employees are being trained ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Output of sports equipment may be reduced whilst employees are being trained ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ amount of decrease in output depends on whether it is off - the - job or on - the - job training ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Skilled employees may be tempted to leave and work for a competitor that makes high - quality products ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ then costs of training wasted 0450 / 22 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2025 Ο²¬© Cambridge University Press & Assessment 2025 Page 12 of 19 Question Answer Marks

2(b) Advantages Disadvantages Increasing automation and technology ΟΔβ²§¬Δ May require fewer full - time employees on the production line ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ reduces wage costs ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Output can be produced more quickly ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ reduces unit costs ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Changes in design of badminton equipment may be made more quickly ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ quicker to react to changes in customer preferences ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Lower unit costs of footballs ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ may allow prices to be reduced ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ price may be more competitive ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increases sales ΟΔβ²§¬Δ May result in redundancy payments for the skilled employees ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increases costs in the short - term ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ legal requirements for redundancy payments may be high ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Maintenance costs of the technology may be high ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increases total costs of making tennis balls ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Increased training costs if employees need training how to use the equipment ΟΔβ²§¬Δ May need to employ new skilled workers to operate the equipment on the flow production line ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increased recruitment costs Recommendation Justification might include: ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Improving labour skills is the best way to increase efficiency at TSEΟΔβ²§β³Δs factories. The skilled production employees will learn more skills which will enable them to produce more output per worker than previously. This will also motivate the workers to work harder and produce more output. Automation is very expensive to purchase the new equipment, and TSE may find it di fficult to raise sufficient funds for the investment in many factories. ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Introducing automation and technology in the factory to make tennis balls in the best way to increase efficiency. Technology will mean fewer production workers will be needed so saving on wage costs and greatly increasing output for each remaining employee in the factories. 0450 / 22 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLIS

3(b) Relevant points might include: Advantages Disadvantages Head office in country P purchases the raw materials for all TSE factories ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Able to make bulk purchases ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ discounts received ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ lowering variable costs / lower cost of materials such as rubber ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ lower average costs ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increasing profit margin / possibly allowing lower selling prices ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Easier to check and standardise the production of tennis balls in different countries as all raw materials are the same quality ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ maintains reputation for good quality sports equipment ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Senior managers could find it easier to control subordinates ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ mistakes less likely to be made as managers are experienced in making these decisions ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ leading to better reputation ΟΔβ²§¬Δ The managers at TSEΟΔβ²§β³Δs factories may not have the skills to carry out the tasks that have been delegated to them ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ so better decisions are made by head office ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Increased transport costs for raw materials to be taken to each of the 10 factories ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ factories may be in other countries increasing transport costs ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Do not have the raw materials available at the different factories ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ so may not satisfy local demand for particular sports ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ reducing sales ΟΔβ²§¬Δ May need warehouses for storage of raw materials before they are sent out to factories in the 10 countries ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increases costs 0450 / 22 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2025 Ο²¬© Cambridge University Press & Assessment 2025 Page 16 of 19 Question Answer Marks

mark_scheme_text: 4(b) Relevant points might include: Simple statements: ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Company B has twice as much revenue at $200m ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Company A has less revenue than Company B ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Company A has less than half the cost of sales, a difference of $50m ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Company B has higher cost of sales at $90m ΟΔβ²§¬Δ The expenses are less than twice as much for Company B at $60m ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Lower revenue means that Company A is probably smaller than Company B Possible calculations: ΟΔβ²§¬Δ The gross profit of Company A is $60m ΟΔβ²§¬Δ The gross profit margin of Company A is 60% ΟΔβ²§¬Δ The gross profit of Company B is $110m ΟΔβ²§¬Δ The gross profit margin of Company B is 55% ΟΔβ²§¬Δ The profit of Company A is $20m ΟΔβ²§¬Δ The profit margin of Company A is 20% ΟΔβ²§¬Δ The profit of Company B is $50m ΟΔβ²§¬Δ The profit margin of Company B is 25% Justification might include: ΟΔβ²§¬Δ TSE should take over Company A because it has a 5% higher gross profit margin than Company B. This suggests that it is more efficient in its management of variable costs. It is a smaller competitor as its revenue is lower at $100m and therefore it could require a lower amount of capital to take over the company. It should not take over Company B as TSE may need to borrow a much higher amount of capital as the company appears to be larger. ΟΔβ²§¬Δ TSE should take over Company B because it has much higher revenue suggesting it is a larger competitor than Company A and will better help TSE to achieve the aim of increasing its market share and it has a 5% higher profit margin than Company A.

3(b) Relevant points could include: Profitability ratios ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Shows the measure of profit made for the value of sales achieved ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ profit is just a measure of amount made from sales ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ but profitability can show the amount of profit made on each meal sold ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Can use gross profit margin which can show if the business is keeping its cost of ingredients under control or if Jemi needs to manage her cost of sales more carefully ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Can use profit margin to show whether the restaurant business is keeping expenses under control ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Can be used to compare with other businesses ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ to see why there are differences ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Compare with different years to see if the business will be operating more or less profitably ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ how well costs are being managed over time ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Can be used to compare the profit relative to the capital of $50 000 to buy the franchise invested in the busines s using the measure of ROCE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ is an indicator for Jemi whether the business is worth investing in or not Liquidity ratios ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Shows the ability of the restaurant to pay back its short - term debts ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ if these debts cannot be paid back then the business may be illiquid and may have to cease trading ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Measured using the current ratio to show the amount of current assets to current liabilities ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ ratio should be about 1.5 to 2 to be at a satisfactory level so the business has up to twice as many current assets to current liabilities and can comfortably pa y its short - term debts such as to suppliers of ingredients ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Can also be measured using the acid test ratio to show an ability of a business to pay its short - term debts not including inventory ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ a measure around or just below 1 is a satisfactory level that shows that a business should be able to pay its short - term d ebts even if it cannot quickly turn inventories into cash ΟΔβ²§¬Δ It can be used to indicate whether the business needs to hold less inventory if it has a low acid test ratio then it may be holding too m uch inventor y ΟΔβ²§¬Δ It can also be used if Jemi needs to borrow as banks can assess the risk of lending to the vegan restaurant and whether it is likely to repay the loan / whether to grant the loan 0450 / 23 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2025 Ο²¬© Cambridge University Press & Assessment 2025 Page 16 of 19 Question Answer Marks

3(b) Conclusion Justification might include: ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Profitability will be most important as it will tell Jemi if she is getting a good return for her investment in the vegan restaurant business. If the return is low, then she may be better saving her money in the bank where there will be less risk of losing it all. As long as she manages her cash flow forecast carefully then liquidity should not be a problem and will be less important. ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Liquidity will be most important as if the business is not liquid then Jemi will not be able to pay her debts and trade payables will force her to close the restaurant and sell the assets to repay the debts owed. 0450 / 23 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLISHED May/June 2025 Ο²¬© Cambri

mark_scheme_text: 4(b) Relevant points might include: Job enrichment ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Workers in the restaurant to carry out additional tasks that require more skill or responsibility so makes the job less boring ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ more customers served per hour ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ reducing average costs ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Workers may become more involved in decision - making in the restaurant ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ makes them feel more valued by the business ΟΔβ²§¬Δ May not want to take on additional responsibility ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ which may demotivate employees ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ leading to poorer customer service ΟΔβ²§¬Δ May not have the skills to carry out the additional tasks ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ additional training likely to be required ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increases costs Discount on meals in JemiΟΔβ²§β³Δs restaurant ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Encourages employee loyalty ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ by giving the 12 employees the incentive to eat in the restaurant with family and friends ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ increases sales ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Likely to encourage lower labour turnover ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ if employee values the discounts ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Lower revenue after discount applied to meals ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ reduces gross profit margin on meals served to employees ΟΔβ²§¬Δ May be too low to have any impact / employee does not want to eat at the restaurant as does not like vegan food Recommendation Judgement might include: ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Job enrichment should be chosen as the business is new and may not be able to afford to give a discount. It might be better to add additional tasks that require more responsibility to the job to try to make it more interesting and maintain increased motivation for a longer period of time. A discount may only motivate the 12 restaurant workers for a short time as they may get fed up with the meals at the restaurant. ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Discounts on vegan meals in the restaurant will be the best way to motivate employees as it will save them and their families money when eating at the restaurant which will encourage loyalty.

1(d) Negative effects: ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Purchasing new equipment / expensive to buy/set up [k] increasing cash outflows/debt [an] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Need to train workers [k] which can increase costs [an] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ If technology is rapidly changing it will need to change often to remain competitive [k] which will increase costs [an] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Some employees may lose their jobs/made redundant [k] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ May demotivate employees [k] Other appropriate responses should be credited.

1(e) Explain two factors a manufacturing business should consider when deciding on a suitable location for a factory. Which factor is likely to be the most important? Justify your answer. Award up to 2 marks for identification of relevant points. Award up to 2 marks for relevant development of points. Award up to 2 marks for justified decision as to which location factor is likely to be the most important for a manufacturing business. This is a general question so there are no marks for application 0450 / 11 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLISHED October/November 2025 Ο²¬© Cambridge University Press & Assessment 2025 Page 13 of 27 Question Answer Marks Guidance 1 ( e ) Points might include: ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Access to skilled labour [k] as may be difficult to find/recruit / could increase recruitment/labour costs [an] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Access to raw materials/components [k] so production is not delayed [an] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Availability / rent of suitable land [k] which could restrict size of factory / increase fixed costs [an] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Good transport links [k] to ensure materials arrive / products sent when needed [an] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Proximity to market [k] to ensure sales/revenue can be made [an] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Type of product [k] a fragile/heavy product may need to be closer to customers to reduce risk of damage/cost [an] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Government grants [k] which could help reduce costs [an] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Legal controls [k] which may prevent the business from locating in certain areas [an] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Access to utilities/power/water [k] as need ed for production [an] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Trade barriers [k] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Security of the area/level of crime [k] Other appropriate responses should be credited. Justification might include: Skilled labour [k] if difficult to find could increase recruitment costs [an]. Another factor is availability of suitable land [k] which could restrict the size of the factory [an].
