4(e) Explain two methods of promotion a business might use for its products. Which method is likely to be the best for a large business to use? Justify your answer. Award up to 2 marks for identification of relevant points. Award up to 2 marks for relevant development of points. Award up to 2 marks for a justified decision as to which method is likely to be the best one for a large business to use. This is a general question so there are no marks for application. Some analysis can be used for more than one method, but only award once. Analysis can be awarded for explaining: ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Advantages of methods ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Disadvantages of methods ΟΔβ²§¬Δ How the method works 0450 / 11 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLISHED October/November 2025 Ο²¬© Cambridge University Press & Assessment 2025 Page 27 of 27 Question Answer Marks Guidance 4 ( e ) Points might include: ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Television [k] is expensive / can include moving images to attract attention [an] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Magazines [k] as able to target specific types of customers [an] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Discount offers / promotional pricing / ( money off) coupons / loyalty card [k] which can make the products more affordable [an] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Social media / internet - based advertising/influencers [k] to reach large numbers of people [an] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Leaflets [k] as this is a low - cost method [an] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Posters/billboards [k] as can be seen by everyone who is passing by [an] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Point of sale displays/demonstrations [k] as this allows the customer to be shown how it is used [an] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Radio advertising [k] which allows them to target specific regions [an] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Newspapers [k] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Gifts [k] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Sponsorship [k] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Competitions [k] encourage customer to buy to receive a reward/win [an] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Free samples [k] increase costs [an] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ BOGOF [k] ΟΔβ²§¬Δ After - sales service [k] Other appropriate responses should be credited. Justification might include: Television [k] as able to include moving images to attract attention [an]. Another method is posters [k] as it can be seen by everyone who is passing by [an]. Television is the best method as this is a large business so it should be able to afford the cost involved [eval] and is likely to reach far more people than a poster [eval]. 6

1(b) Relevant points might include: Advantages Disadvantages Telephone calls ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Speaks directly to each supplier of ribbons ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ gets immediate feedback ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ so knows the message has been received ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Can verify if the message for the order of fresh flowers is understood ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ answer questions about the orders ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ to ensure the correct flowers are delivered ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ possibly meeting customer orders more quickly ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Know the message has been received ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ with having a good relationship with the supplier which means they will answer and take the order ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Suppliers may not answer ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ delay in order being taken ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ delivery of flowers may be delayed ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ which slows production and may be late being delivered to a wedding ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Poor connection over the network used ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ may distort the message and make it difficult to understand ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ resulting in wrong order being made ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ which may lead to under or over ordered flowers ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Could take a long time to call suppliers ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ may not answer straight away and may need to call several times taking time that could be used for something else ΟΔβ²§¬Δ No permanent record of the conversation ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ if an incorrect order is made there is no proof the supplier made a mistake 0450 / 22 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLISHED October/November 2025 Ο²¬© Cambridge University Press & Assessment 2025 Page 9 of 22 Question Answer Marks

1(b) Advantages Disadvantages Emails ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Provides a written record for FF to refer to ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ if the suppliers do not meet the order correctly and the wrong flowers are delivered ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Can be referred to at different times to check on the amount of perishable supplies ordered to ensure the correct order is delivered on the correct date ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Kathryn does not have to be in the office to send the order ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ can be sent out of usual office hours ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ may be more convenient especially as FF is a small business ΟΔβ²§¬Δ Some suppliers may not have access to a reliable internet connection ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ so FF may not know the order for ribbons has not been received ΟΔβ²§¬Δ The order may not be seen if there are too many emails to read and get lost or missed ΟΔβ²§¬Δ May go into junk ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ so supplier of packaging is unaware there is an order Recommendation Justification might include: ΟΔβ²§¬Δ A telephone call should be made if the order for ribbons is needed quickly to ensure the message is received. Feedback can clarify that the correct order has been made. An email may get lost amongst many emails and the urgency may not be realised, therefor e making the order late being processed and delivery of the flower arrangements for the wedding being delayed. ΟΔβ²§¬Δ An email should be used as it is sent straight away, and the supplier of fresh flowers can reply letting FF know that the order has been received and if it can be dispatched straight away. 0450 / 22 Cambridge IGCSE ΟΔβ²§βÄ€ Mark Scheme PUBLISHED October/November 2025 Ο²¬© Cambridge University Press & Assessment 2025 Page 10 of 22 Question Answer Marks 2 ( a ) Explain two benefits and two limitations of a business operating in a niche market.
